name: Acute tadalafil did not improve VO2max or exercise thresholds in healthy male athletes
statement: In 14 healthy male athletes exercising in normoxia, single-dose tadalafil 20 mg did not modify VO2max, individual ventilatory threshold, or individual anaerobic threshold compared with placebo, despite lowering systolic blood pressure before and after exercise.
population_or_context: Healthy male athletes during maximal cycle ergometer testing in normoxia.
quantitative_note: VO2/HR at individual ventilatory threshold was lower with tadalafil (13.3 +/- 1.8 vs 14.5 +/- 2.1 mL/beat; P=0.03); VO2max, IVT, and IAT were not modified; systolic BP was lower before and after exercise (P<0.05).

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports crossover design, outcomes, BP changes, and null VO2max/threshold findings.

caveats: Does not address chronic daily low-dose use or altitude.
population_profile: Healthy trained male athletes; moderate directness for acute performance claims in healthy men, not for broader physical function.
endpoint_measure: VO2max, ventilatory threshold, anaerobic threshold, oxygen pulse, blood pressure.
effect_estimate: No change in VO2max, IVT, or IAT; VO2/HR lower at IVT and systolic BP lower before/after exercise.
safety_findings: Lower systolic BP around exercise may matter in heat, dehydration, alcohol, antihypertensive, or nitrate contexts.
directness_to_target_population: Moderate for acute normoxic exercise testing in healthy men; low for chronic daily use or untrained men.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Small sample, crossover design, acute exposure, and exercise-test endpoints only.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; no class transfer required, but the acute 20 mg athlete setting is narrow.
